paper_title: Opportunity analysis of agriculture products in Sri Lanka: Measuring markets and feasibility
research_answer: East Asia (China, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Hong Kong) represents 15.4% of world imports for selected agricultural products — significantly smaller than the EU (42%) and US (18.8%), but still a substantial market at $14.9 billion. Its product composition differs meaningfully from Western markets. The largest East Asian markets are concentrated in seafood — Shrimp and Cuttlefish/Octopus & Bivalves — rather than fruits, vegetables, or spices that dominate Western demand. Three products are primarily oriented toward East Asia rather than other markets: Cassava, Live Fish, and Sea Cucumber. By growth rate, Pineapple, Cinnamon, Vanilla, Plantains & Bananas, Live Plants, and Strawberries are growing fastest in East Asia. However, a key warning: the Pepper market is shrinking in East Asia, and Sea Cucumber — despite being a targeted development product — also has a shrinking East Asian market. This market therefore rewards seafood exporters and some tropical fruits while being a poor fit for spice exporters focused on Pepper.
supporting_quotes: East Asian Market (China, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Hong Kong) 14.9 [USD, bn], 15.4% [of world]
The largest East Asian markets are for Shrimp and Cuttlefish, Octopus & Bivalves.
East Asia: Cassava, Live Fish, and Sea Cucumber
While Pineapple, Cinnamon, Vanilla, Plantains & Bananas, Live Plants, and Strawberries have the highest growth rates [in East Asia].
Pepper: Moderate size and high-to-moderate growth in EU, US, ME; Shrinking in EA
MoPI targeted the development of the Sea Cucumber projects for the East Asian market. However, this product has a shrinking market in East Asia, which should be considered.

data_points.product: Pineapple, Cinnamon, Vanilla, Plantains & Bananas, Live Plants, Strawberries
